name: smbus2 (I2C/SMBus Communication)

auth.notes: Local I2C bus; no auth; Linux /dev/i2c-X device access

performance.notes: Hardware I2C bus speed: 100/400/1000 kHz; Python overhead minimal

agent_readiness.notes: Python I2C/SMBus access for Raspberry Pi and Linux SBCs. from smbus2 import SMBus. with SMBus(1) as bus: val = bus.read_byte_data(0x48, 0x00). bus.write_byte_data(address, register, value). bus.read_i2c_block_data(addr, reg, length) → list of bytes. bus.write_i2c_block_data(addr, reg, [byte1, byte2]). SMBus(1) → /dev/i2c-1. SMBusWrapper: with SMBusWrapper(1) as bus. read_word_data, write_word_data. i2c_msg: msg = i2c_msg.read(addr, 2); bus.i2c_rdwr(msg). Process call. Block process call. Context manager auto-closes. Device address 7-bit (0x48 etc.).

gotchas: Linux only — no Windows or macOS support (use ftdi or ch341 adapters for PC)
User must be in i2c group or run as root on Linux
Read-then-write race condition on multi-master buses
Clock stretching bugs exist on some Raspberry Pi I2C hardware revisions
Device addresses are 7-bit — left-shifted by 1 in some datasheets (use 0x48 not 0x90)
best_when: Raspberry Pi / SBC I2C sensor reading (temperature, pressure, IMU, OLED)
When direct register read/write to I2C peripherals is needed
Prototyping with Arduino-I2C-compatible sensors from Python
avoid_when: Non-Linux platforms (use CH341/FTDI USB-I2C adapters with different libraries)
Production embedded systems (use C/C++ for deterministic timing)
summary: smbus2 provides I2C/SMBus communication on Linux (Raspberry Pi, SBCs). SMBus(1).read_byte_data(0x48, 0x00). write_byte_data(). read_i2c_block_data(). i2c_rdwr() for combined messages. Context manager. MIT by Karl-Petter Lindegaard. Best for RPi sensor I2C: temperature, pressure, IMU, OLED. Linux only. User needs i2c group. 7-bit addresses. No async. Clock stretching bugs on some RPi revisions.
